title: The small ones go back
summary: On a fishing boat near Sicily, a boy asks his father why he throws so many fish back into the sea.

body.speaker: Matteo
body.text: Papa, you caught a lot today. Why are you throwing some back?

body.speaker: Salvatore
body.text: Look at this one. It is small. See its size in my hand? It is too young.

body.speaker: Matteo
body.text: But a fish is a fish. The market will buy it.

body.speaker: Salvatore
body.text: The market will buy anything. The market does not care. The sea cares.

body.speaker: Matteo
body.text: The sea does not talk, Papa.

body.speaker: Salvatore
body.text: The sea talks. But you must listen for many years. When I was a boy, my father caught fish like trees. Big. Many. Now the fish are smaller. The boats are bigger. The nets are bigger. The sea is the same sea.

body.speaker: Matteo
body.text: So if you keep the small ones, there are no big ones later?

body.speaker: Salvatore
body.text: Yes. A small fish today is a big fish in two years. If I take it now, I steal from two years from now. I steal from you.

body.speaker: Salvatore
body.text: You will have this boat one day. If the sea is empty, the boat is empty. The boat is only wood and a motor. The sea is the work.

body.speaker: Matteo
body.text: The other fishermen keep the small ones.

body.speaker: Salvatore
body.text: I know. That is their choice. This is my choice. On this boat, the small ones go back.

body.speaker: Matteo
body.text: Can I throw the next one back?

body.speaker: Salvatore
body.text: Yes. Hold it gently. Two hands. Do not drop it from high. Lower it to the water. Say nothing. Just let it go.

annotations.meaningVi: nhỏ hơn (so sánh hơn của small)
ăn cắp / lấy đi không xin phép
thả nó ra / buông tay (cụm cố định)

grammarNotes.title: First conditional with 'if'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: Cấu trúc 'If + present simple, present/future' dùng để nói về một điều kiện thật có thể xảy ra. Trong bài: 'If I take it now, I steal from two years from now.' và 'If the sea is empty, the boat is empty.' Mệnh đề if dùng hiện tại đơn, mệnh đề chính cũng hiện tại đơn vì nói về quy luật/hệ quả luôn đúng.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: The structure 'If + present simple, present/future' describes a real, possible condition. In the dialogue: 'If I take it now, I steal from two years from now.' and 'If the sea is empty, the boat is empty.' The if-clause uses present simple, and the main clause also uses present simple here because the speaker is stating a general rule.

translationVi:
Matteo: Papa, hôm nay cha bắt được nhiều cá. Sao cha lại ném vài con xuống?
Salvatore: Nhìn con này. Nó nhỏ. Thấy nó nằm gọn trong tay cha không? Nó còn quá non.
Matteo: Nhưng cá thì vẫn là cá. Chợ sẽ mua mà.
Salvatore: Chợ thì mua gì cũng được. Chợ không quan tâm. Biển mới quan tâm.
Matteo: Biển có nói được đâu cha.
Salvatore: Biển nói chứ. Nhưng phải lắng nghe nhiều năm. Hồi cha còn bé, ông nội bắt cá to như cây. To. Nhiều. Giờ cá nhỏ hơn. Tàu thì to hơn. Lưới to hơn. Biển vẫn là biển cũ.
Matteo: Vậy nếu mình giữ con nhỏ, sau này không còn con to?
Salvatore: Đúng. Một con cá nhỏ hôm nay là một con cá to sau hai năm. Cha lấy bây giờ là cha ăn cắp của hai năm sau. Cha ăn cắp của con.
Matteo: Của con sao?
Salvatore: Sau này con sẽ có chiếc tàu này. Nếu biển trống, tàu cũng trống. Tàu chỉ là gỗ với cái máy. Biển mới là công việc.
Matteo: Mấy người đánh cá khác giữ con nhỏ mà.
Salvatore: Cha biết. Đó là lựa chọn của họ. Đây là lựa chọn của cha. Trên tàu này, con nhỏ thì thả về.
Matteo: Con thả con tiếp theo được không?
Salvatore: Được. Cầm nhẹ thôi. Hai tay. Đừng thả từ trên cao. Hạ sát mặt nước. Đừng nói gì. Chỉ thả ra thôi.

questions.explanation: The father is throwing fish back into the sea from a boat.

questions.prompt: Why does Salvatore throw some fish back?
options: They are too small / too young
questions.explanation: 'It is small. See its size in my hand? It is too young.'

questions.prompt: What does Matteo say about the market?
questions.explanation: 'But a fish is a fish. The market will buy it.'

questions.prompt: What does Salvatore say cares about the fish?
questions.explanation: 'The market does not care. The sea cares.'

questions.prompt: How were the fish in his father's time?
questions.explanation: 'My father caught fish like trees. Big. Many.'

questions.prompt: Why does Salvatore say he 'steals' if he keeps a small fish?
options: Because it would have been a big fish in two years
Because small fish belong to the market
Because he did not pay for it
Because the boat is not his
questions.explanation: 'A small fish today is a big fish in two years. If I take it now, I steal from two years from now.'

questions.prompt: What will Matteo have one day?
questions.explanation: 'You will have this boat one day.'

questions.prompt: What do the other fishermen do?
questions.explanation: 'The other fishermen keep the small ones.'

questions.prompt: How does Salvatore tell Matteo to release the fish?
options: Hold it gently with two hands and lower it to the water
Throw it as far as possible
questions.explanation: 'Hold it gently. Two hands. Do not drop it from high. Lower it to the water.'

questions.prompt: What is the main idea of the conversation?
options: A small choice today protects the work of tomorrow.
The market is the most important thing.
questions.explanation: The father's reasoning ties the small fish to the future boat and to Matteo.
questions.hint: Think about why the small ones go back.

Matteo: From me?

blanks.explanation: If you keep the small ones.
A big fish in two years.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Salvatore says, 'If I take it now, I steal from two years from now.' Is there a small thing in your own life — a choice, a habit, a purchase — that you might be quietly 'stealing' from a future version of yourself?
